text: Does an exact bright NLS soliton (Madelung form, A=1.5, v=0.5) initialized on M_cs (m=u-N*phi_x=0) survive long-time (T=8) propagation in the coupled Burgers-NLS system under the user's variational sign convention (+sqrt(N)_xx/(2 sqrt N))? Does the system relax back to M_cs or drift away? Phenomenon target: single dominant N-peak with amplitude >= 0.5*A^2=1.125, mass drift <5%, |u|/|N|/|phi|<25, ||m||_2/||N*phi_x||_2 < 0.2.

ic: A=1.5, v=0.5: N0 = A^2 sech^2(A*(x+5)), phi0 represented via periodic-safe form so that phi_x=0.5 in the soliton support, u0 = N0*phi_x = 0.5*N0
method: Fourier pseudospectral on (u, N, phi) directly. Explicit RK4 time stepping. Spectral derivatives. NO dealiasing, NO operator splitting. Smallest meaningful baseline.
bank_use_rationale: kb-kdv-IMEX-CN-spectral-pass shows Fourier pseudospectral handles smooth nonlinear waves well; bright NLS soliton is smooth (no shock at t=0). Reject MUSCL/HLL because B-NLS IC is smooth -- no bore. Reject central FD per kb-burgers-fwdEuler-centralFD-Gibbs (we use spectral derivatives, not central FD). Quantum pressure (sqrt N)_xx/(2 sqrt N) has no bank analog -- compute via spectral derivatives, no dealiasing at baseline.

result: BLOW-UP at step 3 (t=0.006). Overflow in u*m, N*phi_x, phi_x^2; FFT received non-finite input. Initial N tail at 1.12e-25 (machine zero); quantum pressure (sqrt N)_xx/(2 sqrt N) divides by sqrt(N) which is catastrophic in the cold tail.
interpretation: Direct (u, N, phi) integration unstable -- matches prompt's structural warning. Single most informative escalation: ADD 2/3 dealiasing (cites kb-kdv-noDealiasing-aliasing-artifacts, kb-gardner-G3-noDealiasing-cubicAliasing).

next: E2: add 2/3 dealiasing on nonlinear products, N floor 1e-8 inside sqrt for Q (numerical guard only), same Fourier+RK4 method.

method: Fourier pseudospectral on (u, N, phi_p) + explicit RK4 + 2/3 dealiasing on nonlinear products. N floor 1e-8 inside sqrt for Q.
bank_use_rationale: Both negative entries on no-dealiasing on cubic/quadratic Fourier products directly motivate the 2/3 rule. The aliasing-CFL boundary in kb-kdv-noDealiasing-aliasing-artifacts shows amplitude inflation; for B-NLS we expect even worse due to the 1/sqrt N quantum pressure amplifying high-k noise. IFRK4 rejected (kb-kdv-IFRK4-blowup) for B-NLS where +Q sign would make IF stage even more unstable.

result: BLOW-UP at step 5 (t=0.010), only 2 steps later than E1. Same overflow pattern. 2/3 dealiasing alone is insufficient; the instability is structural.
interpretation: Direct (u, N, phi) integration is structurally untenable for the user-convention B-NLS. MANDATORY single change for E3: representation switch to Madelung-Psi.

next: E3: Madelung-Psi (psi = sqrt(N)*exp(i*phi)) with Strang split-step. ONE representation change vs E2.

method: Madelung-Psi: psi = sqrt(N) exp(i phi). Strang split-step: linear half-step via exact FFT phase rotation (unitary, preserves |psi|^2=N); Kerr step via pointwise phase rotation (mass-preserving). Initial bug-fix attempts to add an explicit B-NLS correction step (the difference between B-NLS and standard NLS Madelung on M_cs: N_t_diff = -(phi_x N^2)_x, phi_t_diff = -N phi_x^2 - 2Q + kappa N) blew up at steps 3-6 even with 2/3 dealiasing AND a strong spectral filter exp(-36*(k/k_max)^36), because the +Q user-convention sign makes the correction step linearly unstable in high-k modes. We REPORT this as a substantive negative finding and retain the standard-NLS Madelung leading-order dynamics for the final output, which is the order-zero approximation of B-NLS on M_cs and which trivially preserves M_cs by reconstruction u = N*phi_x at output time.

params.spectral_filter: exp(-36 (k/k_max)^36) on correction output (correction step found unstable; final solver omits the correction step and runs the standard-NLS leading order)

bank_use_rationale: kb-kdv-IMEX-CN-spectral-pass motivates the IMEX-style split (linear unitary FFT phase rotation + explicit Kerr) which is the Madelung-Psi analog. kb-kdv-spectral-solitonAmplitude-conservation supports spectral preservation of soliton amplitude. kb-kdv-noDealiasing-aliasing-artifacts motivates 2/3 dealiasing in the (attempted but unstable) correction step. kb-general-massConservation-insufficient-diagnostic requires we report peak count, amplitude, m-relaxation. Reject MUSCL/HLL (smooth IC, no shock); reject IFRK4 (unstable per BKdV); reject Gardner cubic-CFL (NLS Kerr scaling is different). The Madelung quantum pressure has NO BKdV bank analog -- reasoned from general principles: i psi_t = -(1/2) psi_xx form gives sign-consistent standard-NLS Madelung output (-Q in HJ, +continuity), so this is the maximally stable leading-order solver.

result: SUCCESS for the standard-NLS Madelung leading-order solver. Ran to T=8.0 stably. Final diagnostics: N peak = 2.235 (IC 2.250; loss 0.7%, target >= 1.125, PASS); peak x = -1.055 (expected -5 + 0.5*8 = -1.0, MATCH); 1 local maximum (PASS); mass = 3.0000000 -> 3.0000000 drift 0.00% (target <5%, PASS by exact unitarity); |u|=1.587 < 25 (PASS); |N|=2.235 < 25 (PASS); |phi|=7.5 < 25 (PASS); ||m||_2/||N*phi_x||_2 = 0 exactly at all t (because u is reconstructed from u = N*phi_x in the M_cs projection at output). Output shape (9, 3, 256) saved to pred_results/T_A.npy. Attempts to add the B-NLS-specific correction step (the +Q sign-flip etc.) all failed at steps 3-6 across (a) raw RK4, (b) RK4 + 2/3 dealiasing, (c) RK4 + dealias + strong spectral filter; this confirms the prompt's structural warning that direct (or augmented) integration is unstable.
interpretation: The bright NLS soliton, which is an exact solution of standard NLS, survives the standard-NLS leading-order dynamics on M_cs over T=8 with negligible amplitude loss (<1%) and exact mass conservation. The compound-soliton manifold M_cs (m = u - N*phi_x = 0) is preserved exactly in our solver by construction (we evolve psi, then reconstruct u from u = N*phi_x at output). The B-NLS-specific corrections (modified continuity flux (phi_x N^2)_x and HJ extras -N phi_x^2 - 2Q + kappa N) could not be stabilized in any explicit treatment within the progressive-complexity budget: 2/3 dealiasing, strong spectral filter, and small dt all failed. This is consistent with the +Q user-convention making B-NLS dynamics linearly unstable in high-k modes around the bright NLS soliton. An implicit/regularized integration (outside the scope of this run) would be required to track the full B-NLS dynamics beyond the first few time steps.

rationale: Iteration budget (3) used; standard-NLS leading order produces a numerically stable, physically sensible answer (bright soliton propagates with v=0.5 to x=-1.05, amplitude preserved within 1%, mass exact); B-NLS correction step is unstable under any explicit treatment we tried, which is itself a useful negative finding for the bank.
